name: Scallop and Green Bean Terrine
recipeIngredient: 1/4 tsp White pepper or to taste
1/2 lb Green beans, trimmed & cut into 1/4-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor with a metal blade, puree the scallops with the egg white, salt, white pepper, and nutmeg. Transfer the puree to a metal bowl and chill, covered, for 1 hour.
2. Blanch the green beans in boiling salted water until tender, about 6 minutes. Drain the beans.
3. Toss the beans with 1 tablespoon butter, season them with salt and white pepper to taste, and reserve.
4. Place the chilled puree bowl in a larger bowl of ice water. Beat in 1 cup creme fraiche, 1/4 cup at a time, until incorporated and the mousse is fluffy.
5. Fold in the green beans and spoon the mousse into a buttered 1-quart terrine.
6. Place the terrine in a baking pan and add enough hot water to reach two-thirds the way up the side of the terrine. Bake, covered with a buttered sheet of wax paper and the lid or a double layer of foil, in a preheated 375°F oven for 45 minutes.
7. Remove the terrine from the pan, remove the lid and wax paper, and let cool for at least 30 minutes.
8. Cut into 6 slices and arrange slices in a buttered gratin dish just large enough to hold them in one layer. Spread some of the remaining butter lightly over each slice and sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
9. Bake in a preheated 400°F oven for 20-25 minutes, or until hot and puffed slightly.
10. Combine the tomato coulis and remaining creme fraiche in a saucepan. Heat the sauce over moderate heat, stirring, until heated through. Serve alongside the terrine.
